title: Fermented Midnight Craving Thai Onion Kanom Jeen
description: A fermented and glassy-jelly dish that's sure to satisfy your midnight cravings. This Thai-inspired kanom jeen recipe features onion salt and a sugar-free twist.
instructions: Combine onion salt, fermented fish sauce, and fish sauce in a mixing bowl.
Add grated coconut and mix until well combined.
Add fermented rice noodles and mix until noodles are well coated.
Heat a tagine over medium heat and add the noodle mixture.
Add palm sugar, rice vinegar, and water to the tagine and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Add sliced onion to the tagine and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Let the dish cool to room temperature before refrigerating.
Let the dish ferment in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before serving.
Serve the fermented dish chilled and garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
